description: Protocol defining advanced workflow execution semantics including branching, parallelism, guards, cancellation, and state propagation.
goals: Extend workflows beyond linear step sequences.
Enable conditional branching and guarded execution.
Support parallel execution of independent steps.
Allow cancellation, early termination, and failure routing.
Provide a consistent state model across complex workflows.

execution_modes.sequential: Steps execute in order, one after another.
execution_modes.parallel: Multiple steps execute concurrently when dependencies allow.
execution_modes.race: Multiple branches run; the first to complete determines the result.

depends_on.description: List of step IDs that must complete before this step runs.

route_to.description: Step ID to route to when on_error=route.

state_model.description: Defines how workflow state is created, updated, and accessed.
rules: Each step may write to workflow state using its output.
State keys must be namespaced by step ID.
Guards may reference any previously written state.
Parallel steps write to independent namespaces to avoid collisions.

types.if: Guarded execution: if guard=true, run step or branch.
types.switch: Multi-branch selection based on state value.
types.fallback: Run primary step; if it fails, run fallback step.

rules: Steps with no dependency relationship may run in parallel.
Parallel steps must not write to the same state key.
Parallel failures may be aggregated or routed individually.

effects: Cancelled steps must not write to workflow state.

rules: If on_error=route, workflow jumps to route_to step.

usage_notes: Workflows may mix sequential, parallel, and conditional steps.
State must be deterministic and fully machine-readable.
Execution engines must respect dependency graphs and guards.
